```xml
Each fact is ONE piece of information

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>

    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.

  -->

</observation>
```

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.thinking:
The user committed changes to the FeatureWheel system. Let me analyze what was actually built/done:

1. Created a new file: `assets/Script/FeatureWheel/FeatureMarkEvent.ts` - This defines an event interface for mark display
2. Modified: `assets/Script/GameState/FeatureWheelShowState.ts` - This dispatches the SHOW_MARK event after wheel landing

From the commit message:
- Added FeatureMarkEvent.ts: FEATURE_MARK_EVENT.SHOW_MARK event contract with payload (type/positions(serverPos)/multipliers)
- Documents the division of labor and temporary fire-and-forget agreement
- FeatureWheelShowState now dispatches SHOW_MARK after each wheel landing, with WheelFeatures Type/Positions/Multipliers
- Currently no handler → no-op doesn't hang; when mark display component comes online, can add done callback and change to await for sequencing

This is a feature - they implemented a seam/integration point for the mark display system. It's an event-based interface where the wheel state emits events about marks that need to be shown, but the actual visual display is delegated to another component (not yet implemented).

Key points:
- Event-driven architecture for decoupling wheel logic from mark display
- Fire-and-forget pattern initially, with room to make it awaitable later
- Seam pattern for team collaboration (演出由他人串接 = "visual effects will be connected by others")

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>FeatureWheel mark display event interface (integration seam)</title>
  <subtitle>Added SHOW_MARK event contract for decoupling wheel logic from mark visual effects</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>FeatureMarkEvent.ts defines FEATURE_MARK_EVENT.SHOW_MARK with payload structure: type, positions (server positions), and multipliers</fact>
    <fact>FeatureWheelShowState now dispatches SHOW_MARK event after each wheel landing with WheelFeatures data</fact>
    <fact>Current implementation uses fire-and-forget pattern with no handler (no-op, non-blocking)</fact>
    <fact>Design allows future migration to await pattern by adding done callback when mark display component is implemented</fact>
    <fact>Seam pattern enables parallel development: wheel logic complete, visual effects delegated to another team member</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Implemented an event-based integration point between the FeatureWheel game logic and mark display system. Created FeatureMarkEvent.ts to define the SHOW_MARK event contract, specifying payload structure (mark type, server positions, multipliers). Modified FeatureWheelShowState to emit this event after each wheel landing, passing along the WheelFeatures data. The current implementation uses a fire-and-forget pattern with no registered handlers, ensuring the wheel state machine doesn't block while waiting for visual effects. This seam allows parallel development: the wheel logic is complete and functional, while the mark display/animation component can be developed and integrated independently by another team member. The architecture is designed for easy upgrade to synchronous sequencing by adding a done callback when needed.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/FeatureWheel/FeatureMarkEvent.ts</file>
    <file>assets/Script/GameState/FeatureWheelShowState.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content.thinking:
The user added a new helper function `HasPostRespinFeature` to `FeatureWheelTypes.ts`. This function checks if there are "post-respin" features (Upgrade or Multiplier) in the wheel features, which determine whether the game needs to enter the FeatureResultShowState.

Key points:
- New function: HasPostRespinFeature
- Purpose: Guard function to determine if FeatureResultShowState should be entered
- Logic: Returns true if any WheelFeatures Type is Upgrade(2) or Multiplier(1)
- SymbolConvert (happens before respin), FreeGame, GrandJP, and None do not count as post-respin features
- This is part of the game state flow logic

This is a feature addition - adding new domain logic to help control game state transitions.

```xml
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>HasPostRespinFeature guard function for post-respin state routing</title>
  <subtitle>Added predicate to determine if FeatureResultShowState should execute after respin completion</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>HasPostRespinFeature checks if WheelFeatures contain Upgrade(2) or Multiplier(1) types that apply after respin</fact>
    <fact>SymbolConvert (pre-respin), FreeGame, GrandJP, and None types are excluded from post-respin logic</fact>
    <fact>Function serves as entry guard for FeatureResultShowState in game state machine flow</fact>
    <fact>Accepts RoundInfo.WheelFeatures.map(w => w.Type) with null/undefined handling for missing data</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Implemented HasPostRespinFeature as a routing predicate to determine whether the game needs to enter FeatureResultShowState after respin completion. The function inspects the wheel features array and returns true only if Upgrade or Multiplier effects are present, which must be applied in the post-respin phase. Symbol conversion occurs before respin, while FreeGame, GrandJP, and None do not trigger post-respin state, so these types are explicitly excluded. This guard function enables precise state machine flow control, ensuring the game only transitions to FeatureResultShowState when there are actual post-respin effects to apply.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/FeatureWheel/FeatureWheelTypes.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

The user is observing two more edits:

1. **Game_Define.ts**: Added a new game state `FEATURE_RESULT_SHOW` to the `GAMEVIEW_STATE` enum. This state handles the post-respin phase after the last scatter show and before the award calculation, where upgrade/multiplier effects from the wheel are applied.

2. **FeatureMarkEvent.ts**: Extended the event contract to include two new events:
   - `APPLY_UPGRADE`: For applying upgrade effects (CASH→Mystery transformation) with payload containing RoundInfo.UpgradeResult
   - `APPLY_MULTIPLIER`: For applying multiplier effects during settlement with payload containing WheelFeatures where Type===Multiplier

```xml
  <narrative>Added FEATURE_RESULT_SHOW to the game state machine enum to handle post-respin wheel effect application. This state runs after the final scatter show in a respin sequence but before the award calculation, providing a dedicated phase for applying upgrade effects (transforming CASH symbols to Mystery) and multiplier effects to the final settlement. The new state was intentionally appended to the end of the enum rather than inserted in logical flow order, preventing renumbering of existing states that might have implicit numeric dependencies elsewhere in the codebase.</narrative>
```

```xml
  <facts>

    <fact>APPLY_MULTIPLIER event carries IFeatureResult[] payload filtered to Type===Multiplier wheel features with Positions/Multipliers</fact>
    <fact>APPLY_UPGRADE handles CASH→Mystery symbol transformation during step ⑦</fact>
    <fact>APPLY_MULTIPLIER provides multiplier data for settlement calculation during step ⑦.5</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Extended the FeatureMarkEvent contract with two new events for post-respin effect application. APPLY_UPGRADE carries the upgrade transformation data from RoundInfo.UpgradeResult, specifying which positions transform from CASH to Mystery symbols. APPLY_MULTIPLIER carries the multiplier features from the wheel results, filtering to only those with Type===Multiplier and including their positions and multiplier values for settlement calculation. These events complete the wheel feature lifecycle: SHOW_MARK displays the initial wheel landing, while APPLY_UPGRADE and APPLY_MULTIPLIER execute the actual game logic effects during the post-respin phase.</narrative>
```

```xml
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>FeatureResultShowState implementation for post-respin effect sequencing</title>
  <subtitle>Orchestrates upgrade then multiplier effect dispatch after respin completion before award calculation</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>FeatureResultShowState executes between final SCATTER_SHOW and AWARD state in respin flow</fact>
    <fact>OnEnter filters RoundInfo.UpgradeResult and WheelFeatures where Type===Multiplier for sequential dispatch</fact>
    <fact>Upgrade effects dispatched via APPLY_UPGRADE event before multiplier effects via APPLY_MULTIPLIER event</fact>
    <fact>Uses fire-and-forget eventManager.Dispatch pattern allowing no-op when handlers not registered</fact>
    <fact>State transitions directly to AWARD after both events dispatched, delegating settlement to existing logic</fact>
    <fact>Seam architecture isolates timing/sequencing logic from visual effect rendering handled by separate handler</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Implemented FeatureResultShowState as the orchestration layer for post-respin wheel effect application. The state executes after the final scatter show in a respin sequence but before the award calculation phase. Its OnEnter method filters the round data to extract upgrade results and multiplier features, then dispatches them sequentially: first APPLY_UPGRADE for CASH→Mystery transformations, then APPLY_MULTIPLIER for settlement multipliers. The implementation uses fire-and-forget event dispatch, ensuring the state machine doesn't hang when effect handlers are not yet registered. After dispatching both events, the state immediately transitions to AWARD, reusing existing settlement logic. This design maintains clean separation between state machine timing/sequencing concerns and visual effect rendering, enabling parallel development of the wheel logic and visual effects components.</narrative>

    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>assets/Script/GameState/FeatureResultShowState.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content.thinking:
The user is reading the AfterShowWin method in GameView.ts. This method is called after the award/win show to determine where the state machine should go next. Currently it checks:
1. If CurSubPlateIndex === 0 (first plate of the round)
2. AND if there's an active wheel (HasActiveWheel checks if any WheelFeatures.Type !== None)
3. If both true → go to FEATURE_WHEEL_SHOW
4. Otherwise → go to ROUND_SHOW_END

Based on the earlier observations, they need to add routing logic for the post-respin state. Looking at the earlier work:
- They created HasPostRespinFeature which checks if WheelFeatures contain Upgrade(2) or Multiplier(1)
- They created FeatureResultShowState to handle the post-respin effects
- They imported HasPostRespinFeature in GameView.ts

So they likely need to modify AfterShowWin or create a similar routing method to handle when to go to FEATURE_RESULT_SHOW state. The logic would be something like:
- After SCATTER_SHOW in a respin final round
- Check if HasPostRespinFeature is true
- If yes → go to FEATURE_RESULT_SHOW
- Otherwise → continue to existing flow

Let me wait for the actual edits to see what they do.

content.thinking:
The primary session added a new routing method `AfterScatterToNext` in GameView.ts. This method determines the state transition after ScatterShowState completes. It checks:
1. If in RespinGame (`IsRespinGame`)
2. AND this is the final respin hand (NOT `IsGoingToRespinGame`)
3. AND the wheel features contain upgrade or multiplier effects (`HasPostRespinFeature`)

If all three conditions are true, it routes to FEATURE_RESULT_SHOW state, otherwise it goes directly to AWARD.

This completes the routing logic for the post-respin effect integration. This is the actual integration point where ScatterShowState will call this method to determine its next state.

```xml
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>AfterScatterToNext routing method for post-respin effect integration</title>
  <subtitle>Added decision point to route final respin scatter show to post-respin effects state</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>AfterScatterToNext checks three conditions: IsRespinGame AND !IsGoingToRespinGame AND HasPostRespinFeature</fact>
    <fact>Routes to FEATURE_RESULT_SHOW when respin is on final hand with upgrade or multiplier wheel features</fact>
    <fact>Falls through to AWARD state for plate0, intermediate respin hands, non-respin rounds, or rounds without post-respin features</fact>
    <fact>Method integrates HasPostRespinFeature guard to filter for Upgrade/Multiplier types only</fact>
    <fact>Designed as call point for ScatterShowState to determine next state transition</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Implemented AfterScatterToNext as the routing decision method called by ScatterShowState to determine state flow after scatter display. The method uses a three-condition guard to identify the exact scenario requiring post-respin effect application: the game must be in respin mode, on the final hand (no more plates queued), and have upgrade or multiplier wheel features present. When all conditions are met, it routes to FEATURE_RESULT_SHOW to apply upgrade/multiplier effects before settlement. Otherwise, it proceeds directly to AWARD as before. This completes the state machine integration for the dual-layer respin post-processing flow, creating a clean insertion point between scatter show and award calculation.</narrative>
```

content.thinking:
The primary session has now replaced all direct `stateManager.NextState( Game_Define.GameState.AWARD )` calls in ScatterShowState with `this.m_gameView.AfterScatterToNext()`. This routes ScatterShowState's state transition through the new decision method that checks whether to go to FEATURE_RESULT_SHOW (for post-respin effects) or directly to AWARD.

This is the final integration piece - ScatterShowState now uses the routing method instead of hardcoding the next state.

```xml
<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>ScatterShowState integration with AfterScatterToNext routing</title>
  <subtitle>Replaced direct AWARD transitions with routing method to enable post-respin effect branching</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>ScatterShowState now calls this.m_gameView.AfterScatterToNext() instead of stateManager.NextState(AWARD)</fact>
    <fact>Routing occurs in two locations: normal flow exit and MaxFlag msgbox callback</fact>
    <fact>Change enables conditional routing to FEATURE_RESULT_SHOW for final respin hands with post-respin features</fact>
    <fact>Non-respin scenarios continue to route directly to AWARD as before</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Integrated ScatterShowState with the new AfterScatterToNext routing method by replacing all direct AWARD state transitions. Both the normal exit path and the MaxFlag message box callback now delegate next-state decisions to GameView.AfterScatterToNext(), which evaluates whether post-respin effects (upgrade/multiplier) are needed. This completes the state flow integration: ScatterShowState no longer hardcodes its next state, allowing the routing logic to insert FEATURE_RESULT_SHOW when appropriate (final respin hand with post-respin features) or proceed directly to AWARD otherwise.</narrative>
```
